$comment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD (FC8 cycle 2) end-to-end: C bit-fields read/write/pack across unsigned + signed (sign-extending) fields, adjacent packing (a:3+b:5 in one unit), a zero-width break forcing c to a fresh unit, and neighbour preservation (the ordinary `pad` written first stays 1000 because a bit-field write is a read-modify-write, not an over-wide store). a=5,b=20,s=-3,c=33,pad=1000 -> 5+20-3+33+0-13 = 42. The exit is LAYOUT-RULE-AGNOSTIC (write+read share the gnu_packed rule, so it is correct on every target regardless of ABI-exactness — D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT). A wrong extract/insert, mis-packing, missing sign-extension, or clobbered neighbour flips the exit. The optimizedPipelines arm proves the flow survives Mem2Reg/ConstFold (the baseline arm is the real-codegen memory witness). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
